@graph.name: Electrical service
@graph.description: Licensed electrical service for residential and commercial properties. Panel upgrades, whole-house rewiring, outlet and switch installation, generator installation, EV charger installation, smoke and CO detector wiring, and 24/7 emergency response. State license {{LICENSE_NUMBER}}.

mainEntity.name: How much does an electrician cost per hour?

acceptedAnswer.text: Residential electrician rates typically range $75 to $200 per hour, with most markets centering around $100 to $150. Emergency or after-hours service adds 50 to 100 percent. Service-call minimums run $90 to $200. Flat-rate pricing is increasingly common for predictable jobs: panel upgrade $1,500 to $4,000, EV charger install $400 to $1,500, ceiling fan install $150 to $400 labor only.

mainEntity.name: Do I need to upgrade from 100-amp to 200-amp electrical service?

acceptedAnswer.text: Upgrade if you have multiple HVAC units, an EV charger, a hot tub, large appliances, or plan to add any of these. Most homes built before 1980 have 100-amp service that limits modern electrical loads. Panel upgrades cost $1,500 to $4,000 and typically take 1 to 2 days including permit and inspection. Newer construction usually has 200-amp service standard.

mainEntity.name: How often do GFCI outlets need to be tested?

acceptedAnswer.text: Monthly. Press the test button on the outlet; the reset should pop out. If it doesn't, the GFCI is failing and needs replacement. GFCIs are required by current code in kitchens, bathrooms, garages, basements, outdoor receptacles, and within 6 feet of any water source. Replacement runs $20 to $40 in parts; an electrician install adds $75 to $150.

mainEntity.name: What size generator do I need for my house?

acceptedAnswer.text: A small portable generator (3,500 to 7,500 watts) powers refrigerator, lights, and a few outlets during outages. A whole-house standby generator (14 to 26 kW, natural-gas or propane) powers everything including HVAC. Sizing depends on what you want to run: refrigerator alone needs ~700 watts; AC unit needs 3,000 to 6,000 watts; well pump 1,500 to 3,000 watts. Standby generators cost $4,000 to $15,000 installed with transfer switch.

mainEntity.name: How much does an EV charger installation cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Level 2 EV charger installation costs $400 to $1,500 depending on panel capacity, distance from panel to install location, and whether a dedicated 40-amp or 50-amp circuit needs to be added. Most installs take 2 to 4 hours and require a permit. Many utilities offer $200 to $500 rebates that cover most of the install cost. Hardwired and plug-in (NEMA 14-50) options are both valid; hardwired costs slightly more but supports higher amperage.
